description: Order is the aggregate root that owns OrderItems and enforces invariants (status transitions, non-empty items); private constructor enforces creation through factory methods
Domain model encapsulates business rules as methods (complete/cancel) with explicit state transition guards, a core DDD aggregate pattern
Money is an immutable value object backed by Kotlin inline class; validates its invariant (non-negative) on construction and defines domain-meaningful arithmetic
Use case interface in the application layer defines the inbound port with no framework dependency, enforcing Clean Architecture's dependency rule
PaymentPort is an outbound port (secondary port) in hexagonal architecture — the application core depends on this abstraction, not on the HTTP client implementation
PaymentAdapter implements PaymentPort (outbound port) and adapts the WebClient HTTP call to the domain interface — a classic port-adapter translation
OrderRepositoryAdapter translates between domain Order objects and JPA entities, implementing the outbound repository port to isolate the domain from persistence details
Order placement follows a choreography-based saga: each phase is a separate short transaction with explicit compensating actions (cancel + event) on payment failure
Order state changes (completed/cancelled) are published as domain events to Kafka, enabling downstream services to react asynchronously via event-driven integration
Consumer checks a processed_event table by eventId before handling, implementing idempotent Kafka consumer to prevent duplicate order cancellations on redelivery
Resilience4j circuit breaker wraps the payment HTTP call to open the circuit after repeated failures, preventing cascade failures to the order service
Circuit breaker protects product service calls so that product service outages trip the circuit and fail-fast rather than exhausting order service threads
RoutingDataSource directs read-only transactions to a replica and write transactions to the master, implementing read/write splitting for DB replication
PlaceOrderUseCase.execute is a suspend function that sequentially calls suspend ports (productPort, paymentPort) with coroutine-based non-blocking I/O
awaitSingle() bridges Project Reactor Mono to Kotlin coroutines, enabling suspension without blocking threads during HTTP calls

codeSnippet:
webClient.post()
    .uri("/payments")
    .bodyValue(mapOf("orderId" to orderId, "amount" to amount))
    .retrieve()
    .bodyToMono(PaymentResult::class.java)
    .awaitSingle()
description: WebClient produces a Mono<PaymentResult> reactive stream for the HTTP response which is then consumed via coroutine bridge
JPA entity maps the Order aggregate to the orders table with cascading item collection and lazy-loaded association, using Hibernate ORM
LazyConnectionDataSourceProxy defers physical DB connection acquisition until the first actual statement, reducing connection pool pressure for read-only or short transactions
Order service depends on ProductPort and PaymentPort to call other microservices via HTTP, demonstrating inter-service communication in an MSA without shared DB
The order domain model is isolated in its own package/module with no reference to product or payment domain objects, representing the Order bounded context

codeSnippet: enum class OrderStatus { PENDING, COMPLETED, CANCELLED }
description: OrderStatus enum models the finite set of valid order states, used to enforce state machine transitions in Order.complete() and Order.cancel()
Companion object provides static factory methods (create/restore) for Order, enforcing construction invariants while keeping the primary constructor private
Companion object holds the fromDomain factory method for mapping from domain model to JPA entity, keeping mapping logic co-located with the entity class

codeSnippet:
data class OrderCompletedEvent(
    val eventId: String = UUID.randomUUID().toString(),
    val orderId: Long,
    val userId: String,
    val totalAmount: BigDecimal,
    val status: String,
    val items: List<OrderItemEvent> = emptyList(),
    val eventTime: LocalDateTime = LocalDateTime.now()
)
description: Event DTOs use Kotlin data classes with default values (eventId, eventTime) for automatic equals/hashCode/copy and clean JSON serialization via Jackson

codeSnippet:
data class Command(
    val userId: String,
    val items: List<OrderItemCommand>
)

data class OrderItemCommand(
    val productId: Long,
    val quantity: Int,
    val unitPrice: BigDecimal
)

data class Result(
    val orderId: Long,
    ...
)
description: Nested data classes inside the use case interface serve as typed input commands and output results, providing immutable value-semantics for application layer boundaries

codeSnippet:
class OrderNotFoundException(id: Long) : BusinessException(ErrorCode.NOT_FOUND, "주문(id=$id)을 찾을 수 없습니다")
class InvalidOrderStatusException : BusinessException(ErrorCode.INVALID_ORDER_STATUS)
description: Domain-specific exceptions extend BusinessException forming a typed exception hierarchy; though not sealed, they create a closed set of order domain error types
Global exception handler translates domain BusinessException to HTTP status codes using a when expression, centralizing error response mapping
OrderRepositoryPort defines the repository abstraction as an interface, allowing the domain/application layer to depend on a contract rather than a concrete JPA implementation
Explicit id null-check and safe-call operator (?.) with Elvis (?:) chains demonstrate Kotlin null-safety handling nullable JPA id before repository calls
also{} scope function sets the back-reference (item.order = entity) on a newly created entity before returning it, avoiding a separate variable for mutation
apply{} scope function configures the RoutingDataSource inline on construction, making the builder pattern intent clear without temporary variables

codeSnippet:
val totalAmount: Money
    get() = items.fold(Money(java.math.BigDecimal.ZERO)) { acc, item -> acc + item.subtotal }
description: fold with a lambda accumulates OrderItem subtotals into a Money value, demonstrating functional-style collection processing in the domain model
@KafkaListener annotation declaratively binds the method to a Kafka topic/consumer-group, with Spring's infrastructure handling offset management and deserialization
Each DB mutation is wrapped in a distinct @Transactional method; deliberately separated so no transaction spans an external HTTP call, maintaining ACID atomicity per phase
ResponseEntity<ApiResponse<OrderResponse>> uses nested generics to type the HTTP response wrapper and inner payload, enabling type-safe response construction
OrderEventAdapter adapts the domain OrderEventPort interface to Kafka's KafkaTemplate, translating domain Order objects into Kafka messages
FixedBackOff(1000ms, 3 retries) configures Kafka consumer retry with a fixed interval; after exhaustion, DeadLetterPublishingRecoverer routes failed messages to a DLT
Private constructor prevents direct instantiation; status mutation is encapsulated behind complete() and cancel() which guard the valid state transitions
ProductPort abstracts the product service dependency behind an interface with a domain-centric ProductInfo return type, hiding HTTP/Resilience4j details from the application core
